reasoning: The SOFR 180-day average replaces daily SOFR rates from 180 days ago with current values. Currently, the rate is ~4.44%, while daily SOFR is much lower at ~4.28%. Rates from late 2024 (~4.50-4.80%) will continue to roll off until roughly June 2025, being replaced by today's lower ~4.28% daily rates. This structurally guarantees a smooth, steady decline in the 180-day average heading into the summer of 2025. The 180-day average will strictly decrease toward 4.28% over the next several months. To increase above the current ~4.44% by August or November 2025, the daily SOFR would need to significantly jump and stay elevated above 4.44%, which would require unexpected multiple Federal Reserve rate hikes. Markets heavily price in either steady rates or rate cuts over 2025, with near a 0% implied probability of rate increases. Therefore, the chance of the 180-day average exceeding the value recorded on May 11, 2025 by any of the stated resolution dates is exceptionally remote, only possible under a black-swan re-acceleration of massive rate hikes.
